Tomorrow, five companies will be added to the Nasdaq-100 Index: 
 
$NBIS — Nebius 
$CRWV — CoreWeave 
$RKLB — Rocket Lab 
$ALAB — Astera Labs 
$TER — Teradyne 
 
In theory, inclusion in the index means that ETFs tracking the Nasdaq-100 will be forced to buy these stocks, thereby driving short-term liquidity and volatility. 
However, the reality is often more complex. 
Share prices frequently rise prior to the official inclusion date. 
Much of the "index-driven demand" is often priced in beforehand (i.e., "front-running" trades). 
Price action following the official inclusion can be volatile, potentially even triggering a "sell-the-news" type of sell-off. 
Several of these stocks (particularly $NBIS and $ALAB) have already demonstrated strong upward momentum. 
So, while this is indeed a structurally bullish event, 
it does not guarantee a one-way upward move in share prices. 
In scenarios like this, entry timing and position management are often more critical than the news itself. 
Worth watching.

With additional tailwinds from major strategic stakes and large-scale capacity expansion plans, the inclusion becomes an accelerant on top of an already strong trend. 
 
This is not just rebalancing—it’s forced demand meeting momentum.

Astera Labs and Rocket Lab shares surged in extended trading Thursday after Nasdaq announced that both companies will be added to the Nasdaq-100 as part of its June quarterly rebalance. Astera Labs, which develops semiconductor connectivity solutions for AI data centers, rose about 5% to $383.50, while space technology company Rocket Lab jumped nearly 11% to $126.95. 
Nasdaq said Astera Labs, Rocket Lab, CoreWeave, Nebius Group, and Teradyne will join the Nasdaq-100 before the market opens on June 22, replacing Charter Communications, Cognizant Technology Solutions, Insmed, Verisk Analytics, and Zscaler. 
Inclusion in the Nasdaq-100, which tracks the largest non-financial companies listed on the exchange, typically boosts demand for newly added stocks as index funds and ETFs rebalance their portfolios to mirror the benchmark. The addition marks another milestone for Astera Labs and Rocket Lab.

Teradyne Inc. has been awarded a firm-fixed-price requirements contract valued at up to $139.9 million by the U.S. Department of Defense to provide Versatile Diagnostic Automatic Test Station (VDATS) kits. The contract covers the supply of commercially available standardized testing equipment, as well as associated components and software needed to assemble diagnostic systems used to support military maintenance and readiness operations. 
The work will be carried out at Robins Air Force Base in Georgia, with completion expected by June 12, 2031. According to the Defense Department, the award was made through a sole-source acquisition process, indicating that Teradyne was selected without a competitive bidding procedure. No funds were obligated at the time of the award, meaning orders and funding will be provided as requirements arise under the agreement.

$TER "Teradyne (TER) has a positive outlook for 2026, largely fueled by its dominance in AI-driven semiconductor testing, which currently accounts for approximately 70% of its total revenue. 
In late April 2026, Teradyne reported "blowout" Q1 results with revenue of $1.28 billion (up 87% year-over-year) and non-GAAP EPS of $2.56, significantly beating analyst estimates. 
The company is benefiting from a "wafer-to-data-center" strategy, providing essential testing for AI GPUs, high-bandwidth memory (HBM), and advanced ASICs used by major players like Nvidia and Amazon. 
While semiconductor testing is the primary driver, Teradyne’s robotics division is also seeing expanded partnerships, positioning the company for a broader automation recovery. 
Management reported record gross margins of 60.9% in early 2026, demonstrating efficient scaling despite industry-wide complexity. 
JPMorgan and Citigroup raised PT to $400 with an Overweight and Buy rating, respectively."

In recent evaluations, Teradyne, Inc. 
(NASDAQ: TER) has demonstrated a robust performance in the semiconductor testing sector, bolstered by a notable increase in its Fair Value Estimate from analysts, which now stands at $139.38.

The EPS growth trajectory remains a focal point, with forecasts indicating a double-digit decline in the upcoming quarter, yet the long-term outlook appears promising due to increasing demand for AI-related technologies and the company’s strategic product launches, such as the ETS-800 D20 system. 
Furthermore, Teradyne's recent 23.6% stock price rally highlights investor optimism, particularly in light of its substantial gains over the past three years, where shares have appreciated by 95.3%. 
Comparatively, the semiconductor industry has outperformed the broader market, with a six-month gain of 69.2%, showcasing the sector's resilience amid economic fluctuations. 
--- 
 
As Teradyne prepares to announce its Q3 earnings, expectations are mixed among analysts. 
While some anticipate a double-digit profit decline, the company’s historical performance suggests resilience, having achieved a 14.7% total shareholder return over the past year. 
Analyst consensus estimates are cautiously optimistic, reflecting confidence in the company’s ability to navigate current market challenges. 
The anticipated earnings report could be pivotal, not only for assessing short-term performance but also for gauging investor sentiment and potential price adjustments in the wake of the results. 
Analysts will likely scrutinize revenue forecasts and any commentary regarding demand trends, particularly in AI and automation sectors, which are critical to Teradyne's growth narrative. 
--- 
 
Overall, the semiconductor sector, to which Teradyne belongs, has exhibited strong performance, significantly outpacing the S&P 500. 
This sector's growth is driven by heightened demand for advanced chips, particularly in AI and automation applications, which are expected to sustain momentum into the foreseeable future. 
Teradyne’s strategic positioning within this thriving industry indicates a favorable outlook, making it a stock to watch closely as it continues to adapt to evolving technological demands.
